message:
native: rms_norm shader ported, byte-equal vs browser fixture

Architecture:
  - WGSL extracted from webgpu.ts into packages/textsift/src/native/
    shaders/*.wgsl (single source of truth for browser + native)
  - extract-shaders.mjs regenerates shaders.ts (browser side) from
    the .wgsl files; resolves ${OTHER_WGSL} template includes
  - Native Zig: @embedFile("shaders/<name>.wgsl") at compile time

Conformance harness (tests/conformance/shaders.html +
dump-fixtures.spec.ts):
  - Browser runs each registered shader on a deterministic seeded
    input via navigator.gpu, dumps {uniform, inputs, expected.bin}
    to tests/native/conformance/fixtures/<shader>/
  - Native test loads the fixture, runs wgpu-native dispatch,
    asserts byte-equal (or within 1 fp16 ULP)

First port: rms_norm. Byte-equal — Naga (wgpu-native) and Tint
(browser Dawn) generate identical MSL on Apple Silicon for this
kernel.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+{
+  "dims": {
+    "T": 4,
+    "D": 128,
+    "eps": 0.000001
+  },
+  "dispatch": [
+    4,
+    1,
+    1
+  ],
+  "workgroupSize": [
+    64,
+    1,
+    1
+  ],
+  "outputDtype": "f16",
+  "outputBytes": 1024,
+  "uniformBytes": 16,
+  "inputs": [
+    {
+      "name": "x",
+      "bytes": 1024
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "gamma",
+      "bytes": 256
+    }
+  ]
+}
